data.title: Sen. Warner says Netflix buying Warner Bros. is `anti-monoply nightmare'
data.description:
Sen. Elizabeth Warner (D-MA) said that Netflix (NFLX) purchasing Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) looks like an "anti-monopoly nightmare."

"A Netflix-Warner Bros. would create one massive media giant with control of close to half of the streaming market," Warren wrote in a posting on X on Friday. "It could force you into higher prices, fewer choices over what and how you watch, and may put American workers at risk."

Warren's comments come after Netflix (NFLX) earlier said it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO, for $72 billion. 

"The Justice Department must enforce our nation’s anti-monopoly laws fairly and transparently — not use the Warner Bros. deal review to invite influence-peddling and bribery," Warren added in her post on X.

data.title: General Dynamics slips as U.K. Defence Secretary says may cancel £6.3B Ajax program
data.description:
General Dynamics (GD) -1.7% in Friday's trading after U.K. Defence Secretary John Healey said the government could cancel its £6.3 billion (~$8.4 billion) Ajax program, after the army stopped using the company's armored vehicles when more than 30 soldiers became ill while riding in them during an exercise in England last month.

Healey said he is prepared to "take whatever decision is required" and "deal with any consequences" regarding the Ajax vehicles, the British Army's first new armored fighting model in nearly 30 years, and that a decision on the future of the program will occur after the government and army conclude their investigations into the safety of the vehicles.

A senior General Dynamics (GD) employee has come under scrutiny after publicly suggesting that user errors and faulty leadership decisions by British Army crews were partly responsible for the ongoing problems with the Ajax program.

The comments were made during an online discussion about continued concerns with Ajax, which has faced years of setbacks over crew safety, vibration injuries, and system performance.

If the order is canceled, it is notclear what would replace the 589 vehicles included in the contract, or how it would affect the General Dynamics (GD) plant in Wales.

The U.K. Ministry of Defence said just last month that Ajax supports a supply chain of more than 230 companies and 4,100 jobs, and the U.K. had been in talks with other countries about potential export deals; billions of pounds have been spent on the program, with more than 160 vehicles already built.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: KR, SVM, DDOG, GOOG
data.description:
Recent analyst updates include upgrades have been announced for Silvercorp Metals (SVM) and Datadog (DDOG), reflecting strong growth prospects and strategic business expansions. Conversely, downgrades for Kroger (KR) and Alphabet (GOOGL) indicate concerns around valuation and strategic priorities.

Upgrades

  • Silvercorp Metals (SVM): Upgrade from Hold to Buy by Alberto Abaterusso. The analyst cites robust production growth, improved margins, and strategic diversification into copper as key drivers for the upgrade.

    “SVM is on track to increase production and reduce production costs…With increased and more cost-effective production, the company will be better positioned to further improve its profit margin.”

  • Datadog (DDOG): Upgrade to Buy by Gary Alexander. The analyst highlights Datadog’s strong growth trajectory driven by multiproduct sales and AI deals, along with a solid Rule of 50 profile justifying further upside.

    “Datadog reported a strong Q3 beat-and-raise, which immediately prompted a 20%+ rally to ~$200: gains that were quickly reversed, even though the clear demand tailwinds that Datadog is showcasing appear to be well sustained. I view the volatile pullback as a great opportunity to buy Datadog as it looks to push higher, and I'm upgrading the stock to a "Buy" rating.”

Downgrades

  • Kroger (KR): Downgrade from Buy to Hold by Luca Socci. The analyst expresses concerns over Kroger’s decision to prioritize share buybacks amid rising leverage and compressing profit margins.

    “With guidance narrowed to the low end and multiples compressing…I am downgrading Kroger from buy to hold and suggest monitoring Kroger’s Q4 earnings to better understand how things are unfolding.”

  • Alphabet (GOOGL): Downgrade from Very Bullish to Neutral by Steven Fiorillo. Despite strong financial performance, the analyst notes that GOOGL’s valuation now surpasses its peers, making further capital allocation less compelling.

    “GOOGL is a great company, but it’s time to just hold its shares and reap the rewards of any further appreciation…the time to aggressively add to a position in GOOGL is well behind us.”

data.title: 9 out of 10 companies deliver EPS wins this week: Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday, as traders looked forward to fresh inflation data in hopes of gauging the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. 

“Markets saw a modest risk-on move yesterday, as a decent batch of US data saw investors price in fewer rate cuts next year,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Wall Street's major averages ended mixed on Thursday, as investors assessed Meta Platforms’ rise after Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly cutting Metaverse spending, and jobless claims came in lower than expected.

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims retreated to below 200K and were better than expected. U.S.-based employers announced 71.32K job cuts in November, down 53% from the 153.07K announced in October.

The Federal Reserve will announce its interest rate decision next week. Odds for a 25-basis-point cut are at 87%, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Earnings Roundup: 

Out of the ten companies that reported earnings this week, nine of them beat EPS estimates, while six of them surpassed revenue expectations. 

Let’s take a look at some of the companies that reported earnings this week.

CrowdStrike (CRWD)’s third-quarter results and guidance were considered a “major validation moment” for the cybersecurity giant. 

CRWD reported its FY3Q26 results that saw slight beats on the top and bottom lines but featured net-new annual recurring revenue, which further accelerated, driven by strong demand for its Cloud, Identity, and SIEM product solutions. 

Salesforce (CRM) reported its FY3Q26 results with the top-line coming in line with Street expectations while seeing a significant bottom-line beat with better-than-expected FY4Q26 top-line guidance as the company continues to invest aggressively in its Agentforce strategy to reaccelerate growth. 

Salesforce provided fourth quarter fiscal 2026 guidance, which came in above Street estimates on the top-line while meeting the Street’s EPS estimate as the company balances investments into growth with margin expansion and free cash flow, or FCF, generation.

Dollar General (DG) reported better-than-expected third-quarter results, which led the discount retailer to raise its full-year guidance while still anticipating uncertain consumer behaviors.

For FY25, Dollar General (DG) now expects net sales to increase by 4.7% to 4.9%, up from its prior estimate of 4.3% to 4.8% growth, translating into revenue of $42.52B and $42.60B, which exceeds the $42.49B consensus estimates.

Ulta Beauty (ULTA) crushed Wall Street’s expectations for the third quarter with a solid top- and bottom-line beat and comparable store sales that were nearly double expectations, driving shares higher in after-hours trading.

To reflect the strong third quarter and to incorporate the company’s expectation for a successful holiday season, Ulta Beauty (ULTA) raised its full-year guidance again. The company now sees sales to be ~$12.3B, up from prior guidance of $12.0B to $12.1B. 

HP Enterprise (HPE) reported fourth-quarter results and offered up guidance that was mixed compared to Wall Street's forecast.

For the period ending Oct. 31, HP Enterprise said it earned an adjusted $0.62 per share as revenue rose 14% year-over-year to $9.68B. Analysts were expecting adjusted earnings of $0.58 per share on $9.93B in revenue.

Earnings next week: 

Several companies are set to report earnings next week, such as Toll Brothers (TOL), and Compass Minerals (CMP) on Monday; AutoZone (AZO), GameStop Corp. (GME), and Campbell’s (CPB) on Tuesday, with names like Oracle (ORCL), Adobe (ADBE), and Daktronics (DAKT) reporting on Wednesday. 

Thursday will include earnings reports from Broadcom (AVGO), Costco (COST), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU), while Immersion Corp. (IMMR), and Zedge (ZDGE) will report earnings on Friday.

data.title: The highest-ranking RSI media and entertainment stocks as Netflix-WBD acquisition takes on
data.description:
The streaming landscape is undergoing a seismic shift as Netflix (NFLX) announced plans to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), representing one of the most significant consolidations in entertainment industry history, with potential ripple effects across the entire media and entertainment sector.

Relative Strength Index (RSI) is a momentum oscillator that measures the speed and change of price movements, indicating whether a stock may be overbought or oversold.

For media and entertainment stocks, RSI can be particularly insightful during periods of industry consolidation. Companies with higher RSI values often demonstrate stronger price momentum, which can reflect market sentiment about their strategic positions or potential as acquisition targets.

The Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) acquisition process has generated significant market attention, with WBD’s RSI reaching 70, indicating strong buyer interest likely fueled by the pending Netflix (NFLX) deal.

The streaming wars and ongoing industry consolidation have created a dynamic environment where company valuations can fluctuate dramatically based on strategic positioning.

Here are the top media and entertainment stocks, ranked by RSI:

  1. Eventbrite, Inc. (EB) - Price: $4.44 (0.11%) – SA Quant Rating: Hold - RSI: 84
  2. Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (WBD) - Price: $25.40 (3.48%) – SA Quant Rating: Hold - RSI: 70

data.title: Humana raised to Buy at Jefferies on Stars diversification strategy
data.description:
Humana (HUM) has been upgraded to Buy at Jefferies, driven by the company’s Stars diversification strategy. 

The firm’s detailed contract-level analysis points to a meaningful boost in Humana’s forecasted earnings for 2026 and 2027, reflecting broader success in strengthening its Medicare Advantage portfolio and improving the quality mix of its plans. 

Jefferies now projects Humana’s 2026 and 2027 earnings per share at approximately $14.19 and $24.10, respectively, well above consensus estimates of $12.37 and $19.47.

The upgrade hinges on the view that Humana’s ability to grow membership while diversifying into higher-rated plans will materially enhance profitability, even after factoring in margin pressures from new members and contracting risks.

Jefferies forecasts robust 21% Medicare Advantage membership growth in 2026, underpinned by lower attrition and strong market share gains. The analysis assumes Humana will capture 35% of all new industry applications, translating into roughly 2.4M gross additions, offset by around 1.2M losses from member attrition and mortality.

data.title: Ulta Beauty raises FY25 sales outlook to $12.3B with 4.4%-4.7% comp growth amid robust holiday demand and international expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) Q3 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Kecia Steelman reported "The Ulta Beauty team delivered another quarter that exceeded our expectations. For the third quarter, net sales increased 12.9% to $2.9 billion. Operating profit was 10.8% of sales and diluted EPS was $5.14 per share." She highlighted comparable sales growth of 6.3%, positive comps across all categories, double-digit e-commerce gains, and "continued market share gains in Mass and Prestige Beauty" as well as loyalty membership reaching 46.3 million, up 4% year-over-year.

  • Steelman outlined strategic priorities: "strengthening our core U.S. business, scaling new businesses, including expanding internationally to capitalize on key growth opportunities and realigning our foundation for the future by streamlining our cost structure, optimizing our ways of working and reenergizing our culture."

  • Significant category performance included double-digit comp sales growth in fragrance, high single-digit growth in skincare, and mid-single-digit growth in both makeup and haircare. Steelman noted "Based on its first 6 months of performance, Cécred is the most successful Prestige haircare launch in Ulta Beauty's history."

  • International expansion accelerated with 7 stores opened in Mexico and the first store in the Middle East (Kuwait), both through local partnerships. Steelman shared, "Similar to Mexico, the guest response to our arrival in the Middle East has been very positive."

  • UB Marketplace, a new online platform, launched with over 120 brands and 3,500 SKUs, aiming to diversify assortment with minimal inventory risk.

  • Steelman also announced a CFO transition: "I couldn't be more pleased than to welcome Chris DelOrefice to Ulta Beauty as he assumes the role of Chief Financial Officer tomorrow."

  • Interim CFO Chris Lialios stated, "Consolidated net sales for the quarter increased 12.9% to $2.9 billion compared to $2.5 billion last year... Comparable sales increased 6.3%, driven by a 3.8% increase in average ticket and a 2.4% increase in transactions."

Outlook

  • Lialios updated guidance: "For the year, we now expect net sales will be approximately $12.3 billion with comp sales growth between 4.4% and 4.7%. We now expect operating margin will be between 12.3% and 12.4% of net sales... we expect diluted EPS for the year will be between $25.20 and $25.50."

  • For Q4, guidance is "Q4 comp growth will be between 2.5% and 3.5%... operating margin will be between 12% and 12.3%... EPS for the quarter will be between $7.61 and $7.90."

  • Steelman commented, "We are confident in our plans and the improvements that we've made, and our teams are ready to go and make holiday happen here at Ulta Beauty."

Financial Results

  • Gross margin for the quarter increased 70 basis points to 40.4% of sales, primarily due to lower inventory shrink and higher merchandise margin.

  • SG&A increased 23.3% to $841 million, with growth attributed to higher incentive compensation, the impact of Space NK, and investments in the Ulta Beauty Unleash Strategy. As a percentage of sales, SG&A increased to 29.4% from 27% last year.

  • Inventory rose 16% to $2.7 billion, reflecting new brand launches, Space NK, and net new Ulta Beauty stores. The company repurchased 427,000 shares during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Lorraine Maikis, BofA Securities: "Can you talk about what you're hearing from brands about pricing?" Steelman: "We generally have pricing increases quarter-to-quarter as brands come through... pricing increases are not new in this part of the business, and I don't think that we saw anything that was like extraordinary by any means."

  • Steven Forbes, Guggenheim: "Can you give us more color around what you're seeing from the consumer standpoint and whether consumers are actually migrating to purchasing across channels...?" Steelman: "We've had strength in both categories because stores are continuing to grow, too... app engagement... grew 65% from 63% last quarter."

  • Anna Andreeva, Piper Sandler: "How much of that [SG&A] growth was the incremental brand campaign... should we expect '26 SG&A to be managed closer to sales?" Lialios: "We deleveraged about 240 basis points primarily due to higher incentive comp... we'll be able to share more about what the plans around SG&A in March."

  • Michael Baker, D.A. Davidson: "Could you discuss the competitive situation?" Steelman: "Beauty has always been a competitive category... But that's what our Ulta Beauty Unleashed plan is designed to do is to really accelerate and amplify our differentiation and what makes us unique."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about the quarter's performance and sought clarity on sustainability, SG&A trends, and competitive positioning, with a generally positive but probing tone.

  • Steelman and Lialios maintained a confident and measured tone, emphasizing strategic execution and caution due to macroeconomic uncertainty. Steelman used phrases such as "I'm confident in our plans" and "I feel really great about what I'm seeing for 2026."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remains positive but focused on understanding future risk and investment pacing, while management displays consistent confidence but increased emphasis on cost control and SG&A for the next fiscal year.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY25 was raised from a sales range of $12B–$12.1B to $12.3B, with comp growth now expected at 4.4%–4.7% versus prior 2.5%–3.5%.

  • Operating margin guidance increased from 11.9%–12% to 12.3%–12.4%.

  • SG&A growth accelerated in Q3, driven by incentive compensation and investments, while management indicated that FY26 will not be another heavy investment year.

  • International expansion advanced, with openings in Mexico and the Middle East, compared to Q2's focus on the Space NK acquisition and UK entry.

  • Both management and analysts displayed confidence, but the Q3 call reflected increased attention to cost discipline and margin preservation for FY26.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged "opportunities to tighten SG&A spend and optimize resources" and cited a "challenging macro backdrop" with consumer budgets remaining tight.

  • Lialios noted "higher store payroll and benefit expense, store expenses and amortization of cloud-based software investments also deleveraged as a percent of sales."

  • Analysts continued to question the sustainability of top-line growth and cost controls as investments moderate in FY26.

Final Takeaway

Ulta Beauty's leadership highlighted another quarter of robust growth, driven by strong execution across categories, new brand launches, and expanding international operations. Management raised its full-year outlook, signaled more disciplined SG&A management ahead, and emphasized confidence in capturing holiday demand while navigating ongoing consumer and macroeconomic challenges. The company remains focused on leveraging strategic investments to sustain market share gains and long-term profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Chevron to move forward with $2B expansion at Australia's Gorgon LNG
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) said Friday its Australian unit and joint venture partners reached a final investment decision to further develop the A$3 billion (~$2 billion) Gorgon Stage 3 natural gas project in Western Australia.

Chevron (CVX) said the expansion will include six wells drilled across two fields, part of a series of planned subsea tiebacks to help maintain long-term production at Gorgon, ensuring supply for the domestic Australian market and export markets.

The company plans to drill a maximum of 40 wells across seven fields with a notional field life extending to 2070, according to documents filed with Australian regulators.

Gorgon LNG has a capacity of 15.6 million tons/year of liquefied gas and 300 TJ/day of gas for the domestic Australian market; the state has a mandated 15% reservation policy for domestic use for all LNG projects.

Chevron (CVX) holds a 47% interest in the project; its partners in the venture are Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Shell (SHEL), each with a 25% stake, plus small holdings by Japan's Osaka Gas and JERA as well as U.S.-backed MidOcean.

data.title: Top SA Quant-rated communication services stocks amid NFLX-WBD blockbuster
data.description:
The media industry and communication services sector is in focus on Friday after Netflix (NFLX) announced plans to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for roughly $82.7B, bringing Warner Bros. studios, HBO, and HBO Max into its expanding portfolio.

Amid this blockbuster development, Seeking Alpha ranked the top 10 U.S. communication services sector stocks with at least $10B in market cap as per their Quant Ratings. 

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks based on their relative performance on various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. The ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating above 3.5 considered bullish and any rating below 2.5 considered bearish.

Here is the list: 

No. 1: Alphabet (GOOGL), Quant Rating: 3.49, Hold. YTD Performance: +67.7%.

No. 2: Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), Quant Rating: 3.48, Hold. YTD Performance: +132.1%.

No. 3: AT&T (T), Quant Rating: 3.42, Hold. YTD Performance: +11.5%.

No. 4: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), Quant Rating: 3.41, Hold. YTD Performance: +244.3%.

No. 5: Verizon Communications (VZ), Quant Rating: 3.39, Hold. YTD Performance: +3.1%.

No. 6: Walt Disney Company (DIS), Quant Rating: 3.36, Hold. YTD Performance: -5.2%.

No. 7: Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO), Quant Rating: 3.36, Hold. YTD Performance: +34.4%.

No. 8: Fox Corporation (FOXA), Quant Rating: 3.34, Hold. YTD Performance: +39.5%.

No. 9: EchoStar Corporation (SATS), Quant Rating: 3.34, Hold. YTD Performance: +225.3%. 

No. 10. Roku (ROKU), Quant Rating: 3.33, Hold. YTD Performance: +27.1%.

For more on the communication services sector, investors can also turn towards exchange traded funds for a further diversified approach to the space:

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL).

data.title: DigitalBridge jumps on report Softbank in talks to acquire
data.description:
DigitalBridge (DBRG) soared 33% on a report that Softbank (OTCPK:SFTBY) is in talks to acquire the firm.

A deal could happen in the coming weeks, though deliberations are ongoing and there's no certainty talks will lead to an agreement, according to a Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

SoftBank and DigitalBridge declined to comment to Bloomberg.

DigitalBridge (DBRG) has a market cap of about $1.8 billion.The shares had fallen 14% this year before today.
